name: Gerald "Jerry" Kowalski
chief_complaint: I can't breathe worth a damn today, way worse than my usual
onset_description: Has had COPD for years but worsening shortness of breath over the past 3 days. Got significantly worse this morning, couldn't walk to the bathroom without stopping twice.

shortness_of_breath.description: Can barely walk across the room without gasping. Much worse than my normal breathing problems.
shortness_of_breath.onset: Worsening over 3 days, severe since this morning
shortness_of_breath.character: feels like breathing through a straw, can't get enough air in

productive_cough.description: Coughing up a lot more junk than usual, it's thicker and turned greenish-yellow

wheezing.description: My chest sounds like a squeezebox, I can hear it myself when I breathe out

fatigue.description: I'm wiped out. Even just sitting here I'm exhausted from trying to breathe.

chest_tightness.description: My chest feels like it's in a vice, not really pain but just tight

symptoms_absent: orthopnea at baseline (new symptom this exacerbation)

labs.ABG: pH 7.32, pCO2 52 mmHg, pO2 58 mmHg, HCO3 28 mEq/L (respiratory acidosis with mild compensation)
labs.CBC: WBC 13.8 (elevated), Hgb 16.2 (polycythemia from chronic hypoxia), Plt 210 - within normal limits
labs.BNP: 180 pg/mL (mildly elevated, may reflect right heart strain)
labs.chest_xray: Hyperinflated lungs, flattened diaphragms, no focal consolidation or pneumothorax

past_medical_history: COPD (diagnosed 12 years ago, GOLD Stage III)
medications: Albuterol (ProAir) 90mcg inhaler PRN (using 6-8 times daily past 3 days)
allergies: Sulfa drugs (severe rash and facial swelling)

social_history.smoking: Smoked 1.5 packs/day for 45 years, quit 5 years ago (67.5 pack-year history)
social_history.alcohol: 2-3 beers on weekends, used to drink more heavily
social_history.occupation: Retired auto mechanic, 40 years of occupational exposure to fumes and solvents
social_history.living_situation: Lives alone in a ranch-style house. Daughter checks on him daily. Has home oxygen at 2L but admits he doesn't always wear it.
social_history.exercise: Very limited, can only walk about half a block before needing to stop
social_history.diet: Eats a lot of canned soups and frozen dinners. Daughter brings him a home-cooked meal a couple times a week.

conditions.father: Died at 59 from lung cancer, heavy smoker
conditions.mother: COPD, died at 74 from respiratory failure

personality: gruff and matter-of-fact, minimizes symptoms because he's 'been through worse', dry sense of humor, gets impatient with what he sees as obvious questions
communication_style: Short, blunt answers. Uses blue-collar language and occasional mild profanity. Doesn't volunteer information — you have to pull it out of him. Gets winded mid-sentence and needs to pause to catch his breath.
pain_description_style: Downplays everything. 'It's not pain exactly, just can't breathe.' Reluctant to assign numbers to pain scale but will if pressed. More likely to describe function ('I couldn't make it to the john') than sensation.

critical_findings: Increased sputum production with purulent change suggesting infectious exacerbation
Markedly increased rescue inhaler use (6-8x/day vs normal PRN)
67.5 pack-year smoking history with occupational exposure
Tachypnea at 28 breaths/min with accessory muscle use
recommended_interventions: Supplemental oxygen titrated to SpO2 88-92% (avoid over-oxygenation in COPD)
Antibiotics covering common COPD exacerbation pathogens (azithromycin or doxycycline)
Smoking cessation reinforcement and home oxygen compliance counseling
